title: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request – Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-210 – Incident Report – Jose Morales / Christine Holloway – December 2–3, 2019 – 6½ Myrtle Avenue, Ansonia, CT

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of the incident/offense report for the homicide investigation of Christine Holloway, initiated on December 2–3, 2019, at 6½ Myrtle Avenue, Ansonia, CT.

Victim: Christine Holloway (age 43)
Suspect: Jose Morales (age 48, of New Haven, CT)
Incident: On December 2, 2019, the Ansonia Police Department conducted welfare checks at 6½ Myrtle Avenue after Holloway failed to report to work. Officers forced entry at approximately 7:30 PM and discovered Holloway deceased in the bathtub, having died of blunt force trauma. Holloway's 14-month-old daughter, Vanessa Morales, was not found at the scene. Morales was initially arrested on December 3, 2019, on weapons charges. He was subsequently charged with murder and tampering with evidence on February 7, 2020.
Case status: Fully adjudicated. Morales was convicted of murder and tampering with evidence on April 22, 2025, following a jury trial at Milford Superior Court, and sentenced to 65 years in prison on July 14, 2025.

Please include all narrative portions, supplemental reports, and officer observations associated with this incident covering the period of December 2–3, 2019. I request electronic delivery where possible. Please provide a written fee estimate before processing. If any portion is denied, please identify the specific exemption under the Connecticut FOIA authorizing the withholding and release all segregable non-exempt records.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

title: George W. Bush Library: WHTV Tapes of March 12, 2002 Meeting Between Bush and President Islam Karimov

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: George W. Bush Library: WHTV Tapes of March 12, 2002 Meeting Between Bush and President Islam Karimov
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to audiovisual records created by the White House Communications Agency (WHCA) / White House Television (WHTV) related to the meeting between President George W. Bush and President Islam Karimov of Uzbekistan on March 12, 2002. 

More information about that visit can be found here: https://georgewbush-whitehouse.archives.gov/news/releases/2002/02/20020221-8.html
An image of the press "spray" can be found here: https://www.gettyimages.com/detail/news-photo/president-george-w-bush-shakes-hands-with-uzbek-president-news-photo/72546813

Specifically, I request:

1. Any WHCA/WHTV video recordings (including raw footage, pool spray footage, b-roll, or “photo opportunity” coverage) of:

Oval Office meeting between President Bush and President Karimov
Any associated press “spray,” press availability, or staged photo opportunity in the Oval Office or other White House locations on that date

2. Any associated shot logs, tape logs, daily production logs, rundowns, or indices created by WHCA/WHTV for March 12, 2002 that reference:

"Islam Karimov"
"President Karimov"
“Karimov”
"Ислом Каримов"
“Uzbekistan”
“Oval Office meeting”
or related entries for presidential meetings with foreign leaders

3. Any finding aids, metadata records, or archival descriptions that would identify the existence and location (e.g., tape numbers, timecodes, or asset IDs) of such footage.

If the audiovisual records are large or not yet digitized, I am happy to first receive the relevant logs or indices in order to narrow the request to specific tapes or segments.

I respectfully ask any video of the Bush/Karimov meeting that is processed and released as a result of this request be uploaded to the @GWBLibrary YouTube page, as has been done with other requests. Thank you, and I look forward to working with you in having this request fulfilled.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware
